browser.description: Open browser and visually verify frontend changes using agent-browser. Use when you want to check the appearance of UI changes.
browser.template:
/browser was used. The user wants you to perform browser verification of the frontend changes.

Follow these steps:
1. Start the project's dev server in the background (e.g. `npm run dev &` or similar). Wait for it to be ready.
2. Set AGENT_BROWSER_SCREENSHOT_DIR to a temp directory outside git tracking:
   export AGENT_BROWSER_SCREENSHOT_DIR="/var/folders/nw/1rhqbntd1118df625z9l82300000gn/T/opencode/screenshots"
   mkdir -p "$AGENT_BROWSER_SCREENSHOT_DIR"
3. Use agent-browser to open the relevant page(s): agent-browser open http://localhost:5173 (or appropriate URL)
4. Take screenshots of the changed pages: agent-browser screenshot --full <name>.png
5. Send screenshots to the vision subagent for visual inspection of layout issues
6. If issues are found, fix them and re-verify
7. Stop the dev server (kill %1) and close browser (agent-browser close)
8. Report the results to the user

commit.description: Create a jujutsu commit with an auto-generated message based on the current changes.
commit.template:
The user wants to commit their current changes using jujutsu (jj).

1. Load the `jujutsu` skill via the `skill` tool.
2. Run `jj status` to see the current state and list of changed files.
3. Run `jj diff --git` to see the full diff of all changes.
4. Based on the changes, generate an appropriate commit message in conventional commit format (e.g., feat:, fix:, docs:, chore:, refactor:, style:, test:, etc.) with an optional scope. Write the message in the user's language but keep type prefixes in English.

5. Once confirmed, run `jj commit -m "<message>"` to create the commit.
6. Show the resulting commit via `jj log -l 1`.
